item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as no source found indicates it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it! Any time I see something like "3% of subjects" I shudder to think about how many people they've subjected to such a thing. Have they really really sent 100 people, or more, to that cabin?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Though some things can be guessed from the phobias alone, I still think there are too much [REDACTED]s. Most of the information REDACTED doesn't seem to have the slightest need to be out. Maybe some blackboxes in their place, or revealing the info, or taking it out completely.

As a rule of thumb, non-information doesn't add to the article. If you know what's in there, and it have no reasonable reason to be hidden, don't hide it.

I will upvote after this have less redaction, or the redaction makes something for the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I loved the buildup here. What I didn't love is when it crashed right into a field of [DATA EXPUNGED] and [REDACTED]. I do like the buildup, though.

Novote.

item.title: Re: Guessing what the [REDACTED] is
item.content:encoded:
Well...
– hide block

Missing some, gaining others that shouldn't be there

item.title: Guessing what the [REDACTED] is
item.content:encoded:
I have no way of knowing for sure the exact text under that [REDACTED], but I think I might have a shrewd idea, given what wasps do to prey, and the condition of subjects leaving the cabin.

+ Would the author please verify my guesswork?
- Did I guess right?

Do subjects leave the cabin with fewer bodily fluids?

Oh, by the way, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think, that being in cabin met once certain girl. The girl, who loved Tom Gordon.

Really, being lost in the woods, wasps, mysterious being… it rings a bell, but just enough to not being a plagiarism. Kudos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really enjoyed this.
Have an upvote, but keep the wasps, okay?

Also: This was the first image to come up when I Googled that phobia.
(Not for those with insect fears.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nearly all Ants don't fly, other than the Queens and male counterparts for one day to mate.

So you're relatively safe from Ants 11 months-per-year.

(I just purchased a Ants nest and a queen. Bit excited.)

item.title: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Version 26 of this article has been reverted, as it made significant stylistic edits without permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still lurk, but life has gotten in the way of writing for the last…couple of years I guess. No related tales yet, or in the foreseeable future unfortunately.

Edit: and to clarify for future readers, my username used to be Trigonous

item.title: Re: Haunted... By cliches.
item.content:encoded: This was written about a year before "11 miles."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted. Good skip, good story, the right amount of mystery to make the reader go 'What is it? Oh, how about it's this? Or maybe that?' etc.!

Just curious: is Trigonous still around? And has a tale ever been written about this?

item.title: Re: First Article
item.content:encoded: Interesting, I like the detail put into this SCP. There are few that protain to so much detail. I'm sure you did the base but, I'm more than sure that lots helped progress it. Still a very nice and interesting SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keep your hands off my [REDACTED]!

item.title: Haunted... By cliches.
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry, but this whole thing just reeks of plagiarism. The premise is clearly ripped off of the infamous "11 Miles" ritual, and the incident reports just smack of that Blair Witch-wannabe "Shrine". And I'm almost certain there's a bit of Evil Dead in there, because that's just what I think about anything involving cabins. Including Night of the Livimg Dead, even though it was obviously made 3 decades earlier.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was kinda neat until I got to the phobia part. I knew the first, but had to read the footnote for the second and then I was all, "Oh, fuck no." I don't know what it is or what it has to do with wasps, but I was immediately creeped out. I spent several years as a child on a farm/ranch. We were picking pears one day and I picked a wasp nest instead. I've had a damn-near-phobia of them since. If the story had outright been wasps, it wouldn't have bothered me. Hinting that it somehow involves wasps? Gah.

item.title: Yay!
item.content:encoded: Just upvoted this to +100.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh God I could've went my whole life without knowing flying ants are a thing.

On an unrelated note, why haven't we shot SCP-006-J into the sun yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Way too many redactions. There is a difference between making me imagine the worst and making me randomly guess. I didn't mind too much the compulsion effect nor the lack of memories after the incident, mostly because you did a good job with the bit about spheksophobia. However, the very last log just killed it for me. It felt very generic and brought down the rest of the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I think the REDACTEDS barely walked the line without appearing "lazy." The author said they have a headcanon for this, and I would love to hear it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't a fan of the article. "You can't find it if you try" doesn't grab me, compulsion effect to enter, we can't know anything happening, long time to get to the point, etc. I actually did think all the redactions and expungements worked though, and it was good enough for me to doubt my downvote… all of them worked for me except one:

Give him your [REDACTED], Doc, you don't need it.

I dunno, I think I might need my [REDACTED] at some point in the future. It's a pretty valuable thing to keep my [REDACTED], you know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nnnnneat.

Upvoted. I wonder what was removed…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, this is good, very good. It makes me think "Kill it with fire!" without making me think "Oh, now that's just bull." That's really hard to do. In fact, I don't think any other SCP has done that for me before. The one thing I'd change is the description of what happens in there. As it is, it steals your…soul or something like that? It doesn't sound very painful.

I regret that I have only one upvote to give this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea and the setup, but there's no payoff description, which left me feeling very frustrated. It feels very contrived to have no clue at all about the inside of the shack beyond the very confused words of one man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My apologies for necroposting, but you bring up some good points. The article is long enough as-is, however, so I'm just gonna address them quickly here:

1) and 2) Phobia gets exponentially worse. Surviving subjects experience total mental breakdown, commit suicide, or violently assault staff within 24 hours
3) Subject's vital signs cease minutes after exiting cabin, no apparent cause of the quicker demise (aside from normal deadly after effects)
4) Human subject is affected as normal, dog does not enter cabin.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seems to me that there's a few more ways they can try to get new information:

1) Send in someone who already has a severe phobia of the dark.

2) Send in someone who already has a severe phobia of wasps.

3) Send in someone who's allergic to wasps.

4) Send in someone with a dog.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding everything Scantron says. This is exactly why I upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After lurking on the wiki a little longer, I finally get the bees reference. Though wasps personally freak me out more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I figured out why this spooks me out.

It's not because of the wasps. Wasps are scary, but not necessarily in writing.

What's spooky is something unspecified involving wasps. Not knowing is what does it for me.

Anyways, reiterating my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ZOMG that looks like my family cabin
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a newbie myself, I hope that my eventual first SCP is at least half as good. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty good first SCP. I think the huge freak out at the end was unneeded, since imo it is used quite frequently, but whatever. I liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And my job is done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you explain this behind a spoiler tag somewhere? I love the article and the expungement is effective, but there comes a point where I freak out not knowing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad at all, pretty good for a first try.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Most people who are actually afraid of wasps use vespiphobic… apiphobia and melissophobia are the fear of bees. I have a fear of stinging/flying insects like bees, wasps and flying ants :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I honestly just googled it, though it is coming up on dictionary.com as correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought fear of wasps was vespiphobia…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What if the cabin was bees wasps?

+1 for the interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the nicer first articles, really grabbed me. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I knew what's in there from the get go, and if I ever do a story it'll get explained, but for now it's [REDACTED] ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very good job, especially for a first time.
You manage to use expungements without feeling like lazy writing, which is notoriously hard to do. The nasty bits of my imagination keep feeling in progressively worse possibilities for those blank spots. Just curious, do you really know what goes there or was it [insert bad thing here] from the get go? Doesn't hurt the article either way, I just want to know more about this cabin and what it does. Which is a sure sign of a story well told.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god, not the bees! This is excellent. I like the disconnect between "it seems like a good idea to go in" and "I'm never going back in there again", not to mention the "Why spheksophobia?" note. I'm glad it's brought up just how strange and specific that is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly. This way it's also a lot easier for people to find just by entering the area.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As far as I can tell, you can find it on purpose: you just have to wander around in the containment area until you don't know your exact position, then keep wandering until you run into it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, I'm thinking this is like some kind of wasp's nest that preys on lost travelers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Congratulations on your first SCP. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Changed to "cozy" to avoid that problem

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you mean homey or homely? Totally different words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's changing, I'm writing an interview log for Agent Gutierrez at the moment.

Also, I think I see what you mean. Should be amnesic, duh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Subjects are completely amnesiac

What. I guess I'm just used to hearing it as something to make people forget.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It can't be found if you or anyone else knows your location, at the end of the incident log you find out they simultaneously stopped working just after the last time their locations were checked. But the fact I have to explain it means something's wrong, how can I clear that up?

And I see what you mean by the climax. I think it needs an Addendum about the interior, I should have known that when I put in the bit about a figure. I'll put in the footnote as well.

EDIT: Added interview log for Agent Gutierrez

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Had to look up definitions but that's my fault for ignorance. Wasps and night time? I'm really curious

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not bad for a first effort, but it feels like a setup in search of a climax, and it sets up too many things that it never really resolves. We never find out what the house is doing to people except that they come out afraid and they starve themselves to death.

Suggestions:
-Put in some footnotes defining nyktophobia and spheksophobia so the reader doesn't have to go on Wiktionary to look them up.
-The agents having their GPS trackers on when they find the cabin contradicts the statement that it can't be found while wearing a GPS tracker. It might be better to say that GPS had been disrupted by solar flare activity or something of that nature.
-"Agent Holland had developed symptoms of sever [REDACTED]." Unless the cabin is chopping off people's limbs, he probably didn't develop sever anything. :)
-Have a brief interview with one of the agents where he describes what happened inside before he dies.

Cautiously upvoting for now because I want to see this get better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm… in retrospect I agree with the resistance percentage. Maybe the time as well, anyone else have thoughts?

Edit: Actually, removing the Agents' black boxes will really improve readability as well…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the percentage of people who can resist it and the amount of time it takes should be unblackboxed, along with the names of the agents. Otherwise, I like it, in spite of the previously-used "cannot be found on purpose" idea. The expungement was surprisingly nice, as I'm actually really curious what's behind it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really dislike the whole "cannot be found on purpose" thing. It feels cliche as shit.

item.title: First Article
item.content:encoded:
This is my first SCP. All comments/criticisms are welcome.

EDIT: I'll refrain from editing the MTF and Secure Facilities pages until I get some feedback.
